mano_cua_sessions.notes: Memory file tree visible but content did not load; Connect to Gateway dialog blocked UI
Boot state fix incomplete; panels dark; could not find Memory tab in full-width layout

Successfully set data-booted=true + dismissed dialog; found Memory tab with Memory/Crons/Skills/Config sub-tabs; saw project-notes and date entries in list; clicking entries showed no visible response in the small panel area

api_test.conclusion: Non-date filenames (e.g. project-notes) are rejected by overly strict regex validation

source_analysis.buggy_code: deleteMemorySchema and updateSectionSchema use z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/) for date field validation
source_analysis.fix_diff: Fix commit 78537087d2 adds SAFE_FILENAME regex /^[\w.-]+$/ to accept non-date filenames in memory directory
source_analysis.root_cause: Memory API only accepts YYYY-MM-DD formatted dates but memory directory can contain files with arbitrary names (e.g. project-notes.md); the validation regex rejects these non-date filenames

ui_observation.click_behavior: Clicking daily entries should open MemoryEditor which fetches /api/memories/section; for project-notes entries the API returns validation error displayed as red banner in editor

reasoning: Bug confirmed abnormal. The memory API routes (server/routes/memories.ts) validate the 'date' parameter using /^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ regex in both updateSectionSchema and deleteMemorySchema. When the memory directory contains non-date-formatted files (e.g. project-notes.md), the frontend correctly lists them as daily entries with date='project-notes'. However, clicking to edit/view these entries triggers GET /api/memories/section?date=project-notes which fails with 'Invalid date format (expected YYYY-MM-DD)'. The fix commit adds a SAFE_FILENAME pattern to accept alphanumeric filenames. API test directly confirmed: project-notes returns error while 2026-04-16 returns success.
